g.title: cluster_2
g.text: Conclusions — what the paper states it has shown

g.title: cluster_3
g.text: Discussion — what the paper concludes from those Results

a.xlink:title: After removing 39 Tirosh cell-cycle genes from the HVG set, ratio of post- to pre-ablation max H1 ranges 0.71–1.11 across six erlotinib timepoints (mean 0.90, SD 0.14); D11 increases (ratio 1.11). A separate 100-permutation null on post-ablation data finds individual-timepoint p=0.11–0.58 (acknowledged as underpowered at n=200–379).
a.text: genes from the HVG set, ratio of post-

a.xlink:title: The cyclic structure survives cell-cycle ablation.

Stringent regression on S-score and G2M-score on 1000-cell subsamples gives post/pre max-H1 ratio 1.07–4.05 across five cohorts (erlotinib D0/D9/D11, PDX YU-006 untreated/residual); mean 1.97; every value above 1. The osimertinib cohort cannot be tested this way because zero Tirosh cell-cycle genes are present in its HVG set.
a.text: cannot be tested this way because zero
Tirosh cell-cycle genes are present in

a.xlink:title: PC9 osimertinib max H1 grew monotonically D0=1.41 → D3=1.82 → D7=2.67 → D14=3.78 (2.7×). Permutation tests confirmed p<0.01 at D7 and D14.
a.text: tests confirmed p<0.01 at D7 and D14.

a.xlink:title: Max H1 persistence is an isometry-invariant, reproducible statistic that increases with osimertinib exposure across validation cohorts of cell lines and PDX (discovery erlotinib cohort showed elevated H1 throughout but non-monotonic, attributable to small per-timepoint n=200–379).
a.text: cohorts of cell lines and PDX

a.xlink:title: Drug treatment reorganises the cell-state landscape so cells can reversibly cycle among multiple transcriptional states including EMT-like programs.
Two EGFR-mutant PDX models showed max-H1 increase under osimertinib residual disease: YU-003 2.79→3.85 (1.4×, p<0.01); YU-006 2.81→6.08 (2.2×, p<0.01).

Treatment-naive patient LUAD (GSE131907, Kim atlas, mixed-driver, NOT EGFR-selected) max H1 = 4.77 on a 1500-cell subsample of tLung epithelial cells. Higher than cell-line (1.41) or PDX (2.79, 2.81) untreated baselines.
a.text: NOT EGFR-selected) max H1 = 4.77 on a

a.xlink:title: Max H1 is quantitatively comparable with — but not directly calibrated against — the baseline complexity of treatment-naive patient tumors.

A timepoint-label permutation null on osimertinib D14 vs D0 (50 permutations) found the observed ΔH1=2.07 exceeds the 95th percentile (1.18) of the permuted distribution, giving p<0.02 (0/50 exceedances). This tests drug-exposure dependence specifically.

With EMT score as Mapper filter, top-100 Mapper-connected genes in PC9 are EMT-dominated; MSigDB Hallmark EMT ranks #1 in pathway enrichment. PDX top-100 are broader mesenchymal/secretory. 11 genes overlap between the two top-100 lists: hypergeometric p=1.25e-11 against 13955-gene universe (operational definition: non-zero expression in both analyses); p=2.1e-4 against the more conservative HVG-intersection background.
a.text: With EMT score as Mapper filter,
overlap between the two top-100 lists:

a.xlink:title: Loop-associated genes are enriched for EMT programs in both cell line and PDX, with statistically significant but system-specific overlap.
a.text: EMT programs in both cell line and

a.xlink:title: With PC1 as an unbiased Mapper filter, top-100 contains only 4/33 Hallmark EMT genes (vs 8/33 with EMT-filter); cell-cycle/proliferation genes (MKI67, TOP2A, etc.) co-rank with EMT markers; PC1↔EMT top-100 overlap is 53/100. This shows the EMT enrichment depends partially on filter choice.
a.text: With PC1 as an unbiased Mapper filter,
TOP2A, etc.) co-rank with EMT markers;
This shows the EMT enrichment depends

a.xlink:title: Pipeline is implemented as numbered modules coordinated through `make pipeline`; applied without modification to Drop-seq cell-line, 10x cell-line, 10x PDX (with mouse-gene filtering), and 10x atlas data; deterministic seeds throughout.

Coefficient of variation of max H1 under five subsampling seeds: 8.9% (osi D14), 12.4% (osi D0), 12.8% (YU-006 residual), 23.5% (YU-006 untreated). Ordering (D14>D0; residual>untreated) preserved in every resample.
a.text: Coefficient of variation of max H1

a.xlink:title: After Harmony batch correction on top-50 PCs, the osi monotonic trend is preserved (1.35→3.94, 2.9×); YU-006 untreated→residual preserved (2.59→5.19); YU-003 effect becomes more modest but directionally consistent; Kim baseline robust (4.52 corrected vs 4.77 uncorrected).
a.text: top-50 PCs, the osi monotonic trend is

a.xlink:title: In 14 EGFR-mutant NSCLC patients (Maynard 2020, Smart-seq2), pooled max H1 grew monotonically across treatment stages: TN 5.37 → PER 7.13 → PD 8.05 (TN-to-PD 1.50×). In 3/3 patients with matched pre/post biopsies, post-treatment max H1 exceeded treatment-naive (median 1.60×, range 1.35–3.25×). Sign-test p=0.125 — directionally consistent but underpowered for individual significance.
An open-source, reproducible framework applies persistent homology and Mapper to longitudinal scRNA-seq with integrated permutation null, cell-cycle ablation control, and gene attribution.
